breadcrumbTrail: Facial Trauma and Other H&N Emergencies
anki.deckName: RadprimerNormal::Neuro::Head and Neck::Facial Trauma and Other H&N Emergencies::Temporal Bone Fractures
deckParts: Facial Trauma and Other H&N Emergencies

breadcrumbTrail: Facial Trauma and Other H&N Emergencies

imageRegistry.caption: Axial bone CT in a 42-year-old man status post trauma from a motor vehicle accident shows an otic capsule-violating medial transverse fracture through the posterior aspect of the petrous ridge, extending to the basal turn of the cochlea .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial bone CT in the same patient, slightly more superior than the preceding image, shows the fracture plane extending into the posteromedial aspect of the vestibule . There is associated near-complete opacification of the middle ear cavity and mastoid air cells.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial bone CT in a 25-year-old woman status post fall shows a capsule-sparing longitudinal fracture in the mastoid complex and widening of the incudomallear joint , consistent with ossicular disruption.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Sagittal bone CT reformation in the same patient depicts separation of the malleus and incus . Disruption of the tegmen tympani is also visible . The fracture plane also breaches the external auditory canal into the TMJ .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal bone CT in a 73-year-old man with right-sided hearing loss and a history of trauma 1 month earlier shows a vertically oriented fracture involving the lateral semicircular canal.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial CISS MR in the same patient demonstrates loss of the normal hyperintense T2 signal in the right lateral semicircular canal , which can indicate hemolabyrinth or posttraumatic labyrinthitis ossificans. Notice normal T2 hyperintensity in the left lateral semicircular canal .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial bone CT in a 1-year-old child status post fall from a chair onto a tile floor demonstrates a subtle longitudinal fracture along the anterior margin of the mastoid air cells with associated pneumolabyrinth in the vestibule.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Oblique bone window reformation ~ 45° in sagittal/coronal planes in a 58-year-old man with trauma shows separation of the malleoincudal joint and malrotation of the malleus and incus.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal bone CT in a 15-year-old patient who suffered a longitudinal otic capsule-sparing temporal bone fracture 8 years ago demonstrates a large tegmen tympani defect at the site of the original fracture plane and middle ear opacification, concerning for cephalocele. Note the ossicle displacement laterally .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Coronal T2WI FSE MR in the same patient confirms herniation of the inferior right temporal lobe through the tegmen tympani defect, consistent with posttraumatic cephalocele.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageEvidence.purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
imageEvidence.duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.
